task: I’ve got to coordinate a few people for later tonight, but one person keeps changing the details at the last minute. What’s a simple way to keep the plan from turning into a mess if someone doesn’t show up or the time shifts?

turns.agent_response:
Keep it simple and set one clear rule up front:

- **Pick one final time and place**
- **Say you’ll wait only a short window** — like 10–15 minutes
- **If they don’t confirm by a deadline, you move on**
- **If they want to reschedule, allow one change max**
- **No hold / no waiting around without a firm confirmation**

A simple text you can send:

> “I’m locking this in for **[time/place]**. If you can’t make that, let me know by **[deadline]**. I can do **one reschedule**, but if plans keep shifting I’m going to move on.”

If you want, I can help you turn that into a message that sounds more casual or more firm.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine, but make it tighter. I don’t want some big speech. I need something I can send fast when people start messing around. Also if one person flakes, what’s the cleanest way to keep the others from waiting on him?

turns.agent_response:
Use a **hard cutoff + backup plan**.

### Simple rule
- **Final time**
- **10-minute grace**
- **No confirmation = not coming**
- **Group goes without them**

### Fast text
> “Locking in **[time/place]**. If you’re not there by **[cutoff]**, we’re rolling without you.”

### If one person flakes
Send the others:
> “He’s out. We’re still on at **[time/place]**.”

### If the time shifts
> “New time is **[time]**. If that doesn’t work, no stress — we’re not waiting around.”

If you want, I can make it even shorter, like a one-line version you can copy/paste every time.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. 

“Locking in [time/place]. 10-minute cutoff. If you’re not there, we roll.”

And if he flakes:

“He’s out. We’re still on.”
